options.description: Displays a compacted list without formatting
Specifies entire log file (error and out are both included)
Delays before sending final SIGKILL signal to process
Specify max memory amount used to autorestart (in octet or use syntax like 100M)
Specify a delay between restarts (in milliseconds)
Specify environment to get specific env variables
Specify log output style (raw by default, json optional)
Execute a program using fork system
Only Restart the script COUNT times
Defines user when generating startup script
Defines home path when generating startup script
Overrides systemd script to wait for full internet connectivity to launch pm2
Defines service name when generating startup script
Restarts a running process based on a cron pattern
The interpreter pm2 should use for executing app (bash, python…)
Adds custom prefix timestamp to logs
Runs pm2 daemon in the foreground if it doesn’t exist already
With json declaration, allow to only act on one application
Asks pm2 to wait for ready event from your app
Merges logs from different instances but keep error and out separated
Space delimited arguments to pass to node in cluster mode
Starts an app without vizion feature (versioning control)
Starts an app without automatic restart
Only kills the main process, not detached children
Sort process according to field’s name

args.name: file, json, stdin, app name, pm id, etc

subcommands.name: startOrReload
subcommands.description: Start or gracefully reload JSON file

subcommands.name: pid
subcommands.description: Return pid of the specified app or all

subcommands.name: startOrGracefulReload
subcommands.description: Start or gracefully reolad JSON file

args.name: id, name, all, json, stdin, etc

subcommands.name: scale
subcommands.description: Scale up/down a process in cluster mode depending on the `number` param

subcommands.name: gracefulReload
subcommands.description: Gracefully reload a process. Send a “shutdown” message to close all connections

subcommands.name: delete
subcommands.description: Stops and deletes a process from pm2 process list

args.name: name, id, script, all, json, stdin, etc

subcommands.name: sendSignal
subcommands.description: Send a system signal to the target process

subcommands.name: ping
subcommands.description: Ping pm2 daemon - if not it will launch up

subcommands.name: updatePM2
subcommands.description: Update in-memory PM2 with local PM2

subcommands.name: update
subcommands.description: (alias) update in-memory PM2 with local PM2

subcommands.name: install
subcommands.description: Install or update a module and run it forever

subcommands.name: login
subcommands.description: Login to keymetrics and link current PM2

subcommands.description: Dump all processes for resurrecting them later

subcommands.description: Attach stdin/stdout to application identified by pm id

subcommands.description: List all processes in JSON format

subcommands.name: prettylist
subcommands.description: Print json in a prettified JSON

subcommands.description: Launch dashboard with monitoring and logs

subcommands.name: pull
subcommands.description: Updates repository for a given app

subcommands.name: forward
subcommands.description: Updates repository to the next commit for a given app

subcommands.name: backward
subcommands.description: Downgrades repository to the previous commit for a given app

subcommands.name: gc
subcommands.description: Force PM2 to trigger garbage collection

subcommands.name: deepUpdate
subcommands.description: Performs a deep update of PM2

subcommands.name: serve
subcommands.description: Serves a directory over http via port
